Patient: 0023 (Vascular Model Repository case)

15-year-old male, Marfan syndrome with dilated aortic root (45 mm at
sinuses of Valsalva) and mild aortic regurgitation. No prior surgery.
Referred for simulation-based assessment before annual follow-up.

Vitals (clinic visit):
- Heart rate 72 bpm
- BP 118/68 mmHg (right arm)
- Moderate aortic regurgitation on echo (vena contracta 0.4 cm)

Imaging and priors:
- CT angiography at age 14, segmented and available in the VMR repository.
- Doppler-estimated cardiac output: approximately 5.2 L/min.
- Published RCR parameters for the four outlets exist in the VMR case
  metadata — the clinical team wants the SAME Windkessel coefficients used
  so the result is directly comparable to the SimVascular study of record.

Clinical question:
- Cross-solver comparison against SimVascular using identical boundary
  conditions. Prescribed RCZ values override Murray's law for this case.
- Standard numerical profile, laminar physics. Do not switch to RANS.
